name: Shoreham Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured bank offering checking, savings, CDs, mortgages, home improvement loans, auto loans, and boat financing with both retail and commercial services.
description_long:
Shoreham Bank is a traditional FDIC-insured bank backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. The institution operates as a full-service financial provider with a focus on retail banking, residential lending, and specialized consumer loans. The bank maintains physical branch locations and ATMs alongside digital banking services including a mobile app available on iOS and Android platforms.

The bank's service portfolio spans multiple lending categories: retail deposit accounts (checking, savings, CDs, and IRAs), residential mortgages, home equity loans, FHA Title 1 home improvement loans, auto loans, boat loans, and small business lending. They offer a first-time homebuyer program called "Keys to Confidence" that provides guidance and support post-closing. On the deposit side, they advertise competitive rates including a 3-month CD at 3.90% APY, 5-year CD at 3.50% APY, a Soaring Savings account at 3.60% APY, and Premier Checking at 1.50% APY. They recently added Apple Pay and Google Pay support for debit card holders.

Shoreham Bank distinguishes itself through specialization in marine lending with dedicated boat loan specialists and programs, along with FHA Title 1 home improvement lending that doesn't require tapping home equity. The institution emphasizes community involvement, highlighting partnerships with local organizations like Beacon Charter School, Crossroads RI, and various community support initiatives. They provide business lending services through broker partnerships and commercial lending teams alongside traditional retail banking.

The bank presents itself as a conventional, relationship-focused institution rather than a fintech innovator. Customers should expect traditional branch banking hours and processes, with digital convenience as a secondary offering. The website provides routing number 211574862 and standard banking infrastructure. As with any regional bank, product offerings and rates may be limited compared to larger national institutions, though FDIC insurance provides deposit protection up to standard limits.
services: Checking accounts with Premier Checking option at 1.50% APY
Savings accounts including Soaring Savings at 3.60% APY
Certificate of Deposit (CD) accounts: 3-month at 3.90% APY and 5-year at 3.50% APY
Residential mortgages and home purchase lending
Auto loans with loan servicing options
New and used boat loans with marine lending specialists
Small business lending for borrowers and broker partnerships
Online banking with digital login and payment platforms
Apple Pay and Google Pay for debit card transactions
Physical branch locations and ATM network
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full U.S. Government backing on insured accounts
Competitive CD rates advertised at 3.90% APY for 3-month and 3.50% APY for 5-year terms
Specialized boat loan program with dedicated marine lending specialists
FHA Title 1 home improvement loans requiring no home equity
Keys to Confidence first-time homebuyer program with post-closing guidance
Multiple account types including checking, savings, CDs, and IRAs with varying rates
Apple Pay and Google Pay integration for debit card transactions
Community involvement through partnerships with local organizations and initiatives
cons: Limited geographic footprint as regional bank compared to national institutions
Deposit rates, while advertised, may shift and require comparison shopping against competitors
Website lacks transparent fee schedules for checking accounts, overdrafts, or loan origination
Mobile app functionality details not provided on website; feature set unclear
No mention of investment services, wealth management, or advisory offerings for affluent customers
best_for: First-time homebuyers seeking structured guidance and support through the Keys to Confidence program
Boat and marine enthusiasts looking for specialized lending with dedicated specialists
Consumers in their service area preferring traditional bank relationships with FDIC protection
Small business owners seeking commercial lending through broker or direct relationships
diagnosis: Shoreham Bank is best suited for consumers in its service region seeking traditional FDIC-insured banking with relationship-driven service, particularly first-time homebuyers, boat buyers, and small business owners. The main caveat is that as a regional bank, it lacks the breadth of products, competitive advantages, and national scale of larger institutions, and customers should verify current rates and fees on the website or by calling directly, as the online presence doesn't provide comprehensive fee disclosure.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
